records.ety: [L. ovatio, fr. ovare to exult, rejoice, triumph in an ovation; cf. Gr. <?/ to shout: cf. F. ovation.]

defs: A lesser kind of triumph allowed to a commander for an easy, bloodless victory, or a victory over slaves.
An expression of popular homage; the tribute of the multitude to a public favorite.
quotes: To rain an April of ovation round Their statues.

defs: A prolonged applause for a person of group after a speech or performance.
